title: Promises & async/await
description: Handle asynchronous work without callback hell.

options: The eventual result of an async operation
questions.explanation: Three states: pending → fulfilled or rejected.
async functions always return a Promise — you must await or .then it.
await unwraps the Promise. Then we add 1 → 6.

questions.prompt: Which catches an error inside async code?
questions.explanation: Either works. try/catch is often clearer inside async functions.
.then callbacks are microtasks — run after the current sync code.
Promise.all fails fast. For 'wait for all outcomes' use Promise.allSettled.

questions.prompt: Which returns the first result (resolve OR reject) among multiple promises?
questions.explanation: Promise.race resolves/rejects with the FIRST to settle. Promise.any waits for first success.
